tweets.text:
Two VLCCs get attacked using the Oman lane. Origin is from Fujairah. 

Fujairah, as the ship-to-ship transit hub, is all but dead.

These tankers are sitting ducks. If Iran escalates into Fujairah, UAE will have to shut-in 3.5 million b/d of crude.

This is on par with closing Bab el-Mandeb.

Spoke with someone who had just made $1,000,000 very quickly, was in LTCG and felt like it wasn’t enough.

I then asked if they thought they could beat 30% CAGR every year, and what they thought that number would be after 30 years.

They said somewhere in the millions.

It’s 1,833,996,950.55.

tweets.text:
Imagine building a small fire. 

Initially you just need the spark, light the kindling and fight to protect the flame.

Gradually, the flame will start to grow on its own, now it’s your job to go out and acquire more wood to feed it.

Eventually the flame will get so large it will consume everything around it.

This is what investing is.
tweets.full_text:
Imagine building a small fire. 

Initially you just need the spark, light the kindling and fight to protect the flame.

Gradually, the flame will start to grow on its own, now it’s your job to go out and acquire more wood to feed it.

Eventually the flame will get so large it will consume everything around it.

This is what investing is.

tweets.text:
Good decisions compound in trading.

> Make money on a trade

> Feel more comfortable holding additional risk 

> Able to size up next trade with more confidence 

> Have excess cash to hedge book and not worry about taking losses there

Winning leads to more winning

thread_tweets.text: September is traditionally one of the worst performing months for crypto, and happens to line up with close to the ~900 day mark post halving, which has been where the past several bear markets have printed their lows.

tweets.text:
Kalshi’s perps growth feels a lot like the early days of their prediction markets & Robinhood's rise to making stock trading popular for retail.

The DAU curve is turning parabolic and already above Lighter / catching up to Hyperliquid. 

When you're on CT & see people use perps every day, it's easy to forget the rest of the US just got regulated access.

I have a feeling we see this number to grow into the hundreds of thousands by 2027.

thread_tweets.text:
e.g. when you look at volumes, it's still early

the majority of users feel like retail prediction market traders taking a stab at perps for the first time

so imo they're not really competing for the same users as HYPE & LIT (yet) - they're creating an entirely new class of users

over time though as they continue to develop more advanced products & their users that are just experimenting with it now gain more experience, volume should start climbing signficantly

same w/ when we see another volatility spike in crypto

tweets.text:
"Every MSTR offering is accretive because it increases BTC per share in a manner that compounds over time.

Fiat shareholders are merely exit liquidity for Bitcoin maximalists in this trade."

But that's not true anymore, right?

Even though they don't want to ultimate redeploy that cash into Bitcoin and it's earmarked toward dividends.

But that doesn't matter either does it?

What's the nuance?

tweets.text: @skumWgmi You are paying property taxes.  That tax is based on the value of the property.  It's not taxed as income.  The federal government can't tax property unless the tax is apportioned. It can't call property appreciation income to  circumvent the Constitution.
tweets.full_text: @skumWgmi You are paying property taxes.  That tax is based on the value of the property.  It's not taxed as income.  The federal government can't tax property unless the tax is apportioned. It can't call property appreciation income to  circumvent the Constitution.
